subject: Fw: AYH - security and elevator update
body:
----- Forwarded Message -----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, March 25, 2008 10:49 AM
Subject: FW: AYH - security and elevator update
 



-----Original Message-----
From: Farkas, Andrew L. [mailto:afarkas@islecap.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, March 25, 2008 10:43 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Fw: AYH - security and elevator update

Pls give to JE. It is an update on capital work at American Yacht Harbor. 

----- Original Message -----
From: Garner, Charles
To: Farkas, Andrew L.
Sent: Tue Mar 25 10:34:14 2008
Subject: Fw: AYH - security and elevator update

Fyi. Please let me know if you have questions. 

Thanks. 
_________________________
Charles Garner
Island Capital
Tel:  212/705-5087
Fax:  212/705-5001
Email:  cgarner@islecap.com

--------------------------
Sent from my BlackBerry Wireless Handheld with apologies for typos caused by clumsy thumbs.


----- Original Message -----
From: Seiersen, Catherine
To: Garner, Charles
Sent: Tue Mar 25 09:48:15 2008
Subject: AYH - security and elevator update

Fyi -



________________________________

From: Petty, Chris 
Sent: Tuesday, March 25, 2008 9:45 AM
To: Seiersen, Catherine; chris@ayhmarina.biz; Roth, Wesley
Cc: Criswell, Ken; Olszewski, Mike
Subject: RE: Security



Hello Catherine,

1.    Elevator - The interior of the elevator has been completely stripped of 15 years paint.  The floor covering in the interior of the elevator has been removed.  The exterior metal framing and door on the second floor have been stripped of 15 years paint.  The back portion of the interior of the elevator has been sprayed with the first coating of an automotive based paint and is currently drying.  Rusted and or dented portions of the exterior 2nd floor metal frame have been faired smooth with bondo and sanded.  The exterior metal framing and door on the second floor have been sprayed with the first coating of an automotive based paint and currently drying.  The third floor exterior metal framing and door have been faired with bondo, sanded smooth and the first coating of paint has been applied and is drying. The finished product for the walls of the elevator, exterior doors and metal framing will be a high gloss automotive finish.  Stainless
 steel paneling is being cut to cover the fiberboard panels at the front of the elevator.  The floor covering will be replaced with a paver brick consistent with the exterior of the property.  The pavers will be less subject to cracking or chipping under the load of dollies and hand trucks.  We anticipate completion of the project by the end of this week.



2.    Security fencing - We have been in contact with the CZM this morning (3/25).  They are currently working on our proposal and we expect a response from them today.  Once CZM approval is received, we will immediately remit a deposit to begin work on the fencing.



Please let me know if you have additional questions.

Thanks,
Chris







-----Original Message-----
From: Seiersen, Catherine [mailto:cseiersen@igymarinas.com] 
Sent: Monday, March 24, 2008 8:02 PM
To: chris@ayhmarina.biz; Roth, Wesley
Cc: Criswell, Ken; Olszewski, Mike
Subject: Security



With the ops meeting tomorrow, would you please let us know the status of the security upgrades, as well as the elevator and any other items that might be pertinent first thing in the morning?  I am sure we will be asked.

--------------------------------------------------------





The information contained in this message and any attachment to it may be privileged, confidential or otherwise protected from disclosure. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any dissemination, distribution or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by replying to the message and please delete it from your system. Please also note that any views or opinions presented in this email are solely those of the author and do not necessarily represent those of the company.
